title: Add character-set allowlist to validate_project_id for prompt injection safety
analysis: The core fix described in this task is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED. validate_project_id (line 29-49 of validation.py) already: (1) checks emptiness and whitespace-only via `not project_id or not project_id.strip()`, (2) enforces `_PROJECT_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+')` via `.fullmatch()` on line 41, and (3) returns a structured error dict mentioning 'invalid characters'. The test file has a comprehensive TestValidateProjectId class (lines 215-333) with 20 test cases covering empty strings, whitespace, injection vectors (newlines, backticks, quotes, braces, semicolons, dollar signs, spaces), trailing-newline fullmatch bypass, valid identifiers, and error dict shape. The require_project_id wrapper and its tests also exist. The ONLY remaining asymmetry is cosmetic: two identical patterns `_RUN_ID_PATTERN` and `_PROJECT_ID_PATTERN` (both `re.compile(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+')`) exist on lines 15-16. The task suggested 'a shared _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN' — consolidating these improves DRY and makes the symmetric intent explicit. A single test should verify the shared constant is used by both validators.

steps.description: Write a test in TestValidateProjectId (or a new TestSharedPattern class) asserting that validate_project_id and validate_run_id share the same character-set behavior — e.g., both reject the same set of injection vectors and both accept the same valid inputs. This makes the symmetric-validation contract explicit and will guard against future divergence.

Consolidate _RUN_ID_PATTERN and _PROJECT_ID_PATTERN into a single _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN = re.compile(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+'). Update both validate_project_id and validate_run_id to reference _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN. Remove the two now-unused individual patterns. All 352 lines of existing tests must continue to pass unchanged.

design_decisions.decision: Consolidate two identical regex patterns into one shared _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN
design_decisions.rationale: Both _RUN_ID_PATTERN and _PROJECT_ID_PATTERN are already re.compile(r'[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+'). A single constant makes the symmetric-validation intent explicit and prevents future divergence where one pattern gets updated but the other doesn't. The task description itself suggested this ('a shared _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN').

design_decisions.decision: Keep the two validate_* functions separate rather than merging into a generic validate_identifier
design_decisions.rationale: The functions have different error messages ('project_id is required' vs 'run_id is required') and could diverge in the future (e.g., project_id might gain a max-length check). Sharing the pattern constant is sufficient DRY without over-abstracting.

design_decisions.decision: Add symmetry tests rather than modifying existing test cases
design_decisions.rationale: The existing 37+ tests are comprehensive and correct. Adding a symmetry test class is additive and explicitly documents the cross-validator contract without touching proven tests.

reuse.how: Replace both with a single _SAFE_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN constant
Follow the same class-per-function test organization and assertion patterns when adding symmetry tests
